title: struthiola tetralepis var. tetralepis
intro: Struthiola tetralepis var. tetralepis is a plant species that belongs to the family Thymelaeaceae, commonly known as the "struthiolia" or "milk and water" plant due to its unique, milky latex-like sap. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues such as coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Struthiola tetralepis var. tetralepis include relief from bronchial congestion, reduced inflammation, and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or taken in capsule form to harness its medicinal properties.
